title: AutoE pairwise-LLM-judge harness ($0, fake judge, no priced spend)
p2_fix_red_sha: 78b28206b1bc8d579d2563056f878e5fa8b11d39 (RED+GREEN squashed in one commit; RED verified before fix)

deliverables.1_pairwise_prompt_builder: build_pairwise_prompt — deterministic A/B/tie per metric; 3 headline metrics + directness kept SEPARATE
deliverables.2_position_bias_order_swap: JudgmentKey(question_id,pair,run_idx,order) idempotent custom_id; run_autoe judges BOTH orders (ORDER_TC/ORDER_CT) and averages
deliverables.3_absent_safe_parser: parse_verdict — empty/None/unparseable completion or value -> ABSENT, excluded from denominator, never a silent loss/tie
deliverables.4_compute_winrates: compute_winrates -> decide_084 primary_per_metric {win_rate,ci_lo,ci_hi,mde,n}; ties=0.5; bootstrap CI CLUSTERED BY QUESTION; seed is a PARAMETER; mde=CI half-width
deliverables.5_bias_and_length_assembly: assemble_bias_controls (order_swapped=True structural) + assemble_length_corroboration + length_contradicts (headline winner loses directness by margin)
deliverables.6_batch_build_integration: build_autoe_batch_jsonl (+ parse_autoe_batch_output) in p0a_batch_e2e shape, fake-tested, NO live submit
deliverables.7_cost_projection: project_autoe_cost reuses d0b_parity_run.project_full_cost; full_calls = n_questions x n_pairs x n_runs x 2 orders

contract_test: test_contract_all_a_wins_round_trips_to_reached_with_surpass + test_contract_all_b_wins_round_trips_to_below_parity — compute_winrates output fed straight into decide_084: all-A-wins -> REACHED with surpass_candidates = all 3 headline metrics; all-B-wins -> NOT_REACHED below_parity
reuse: BiasControls / LengthCorroboration / HEADLINE_METRICS imported from eval.decision_rule_084
BaseAnswerer / Hit / RetrievalAdapter imported from eval.r2_parity_eval (build_arm_answers)

gates.agent_typecheck: EXIT 0 (pyright: 0 errors, 46 pre-existing native-extension import warnings)
gates.pytest: PASS 27/27 (FATHOMDB_TESTS_NO_REBUILD=1, tests/test_autoe_judge.py) — was 26, +1 for the P2 resume-rejudge test

codex_review_fixes.P2_rejudge_fully_absent_on_resume: run_autoe stored a failed (empty/None/unparseable) completion as a FULLY-ABSENT Judgment; on resume `if key in out: continue` cached it as done and never re-judged it, violating the 'ABSENT re-attempted on resume' resilience contract. FIX: added _is_fully_absent(judgment, metrics) and changed the skip to `if key in out and not _is_fully_absent(out[key], metrics): continue` — dead cells (every metric in the run's `metrics` is ABSENT) are re-judged; a cell with >=1 decided metric is kept. TDD: test_run_autoe_rejudges_fully_absent_cell_on_resume (CountingJudge asserts calls==1 and the decided cell is untouched). Commit 78b28206.

deviations: Added parse_autoe_batch_output (counterpart of build_autoe_batch_jsonl) — small, in-scope for deliverable 6 (batch integration point); ABSENT-safe per cid, supports resume round-trip. Not a scope change to the rule.
Added build_arm_answers helper to satisfy 'reuse the answerer/Hit seam' explicitly (imports BaseAnswerer/Hit/RetrievalAdapter); answers are produced via the shared identical-answerer.

Bootstrap uses stdlib random.Random(f'{seed}:{metric}') (not numpy) to keep the module dependency-light and build-independent, mirroring decision_rule_084's pure-stdlib ethos. Seed is a parameter; deterministic.
out_of_scope_untouched: no real judge/pilot run; no embedder upgrade
notes_for_orchestrator: Reviewer (codex) + cherry-pick run after this exits. The priced AutoE pilot that drives this harness is a later HITL-gated step; project_autoe_cost produces the dry-run USD number for that gate.
